title: Codeograph Knowledge Graph
description: Deterministic property-graph produced by the AST pass. Contains nodes[] and edges[]. This file is canonical-form serialised (sorted keys, sorted arrays, LF line endings, no timestamps or run IDs) so sha256 digests are byte-stable across identical runs.

nodes.description: All graph nodes. Discriminated by the 'kind' property.

edges.description: All graph edges. Discriminated by the 'kind' property.

ModuleNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name (string), build_tool (enum: maven|gradle), source_roots (string[]), pom_path (string). What else does a graph consumer need to know about a module?

ClassNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name, modifiers (public/abstract/final/static), stereotype (Spring annotation: @Service/@Repository/@RestController/@Component/@Configuration/@Entity or null), superclass (fqcn or null), interfaces (fqcn[]), is_inner_class (bool), source_file, line_range ([start,end]). Class-level complexity from ADR-004: wmc (int), cbo (int), lcom4 (int).

InterfaceNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name, modifiers, extends_interfaces (fqcn[]), source_file, line_range. Note: InterfaceNode and ClassNode share several fields — consider whether a shared $defs base is worth the complexity for v1.

EnumNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name, modifiers, constants (string[]), implements_interfaces (fqcn[]), source_file, line_range.

RecordNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name, components ([{name, type}]), implements_interfaces (fqcn[]), source_file, line_range.

AnnotationTypeNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name, modifiers, elements ([{name, type, default}]), source_file, line_range.

MethodNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name, return_type (string), parameters ([{name, type}]), modifiers, annotations (string[]), is_constructor (bool), line_range. Method-level complexity from ADR-004 lives here (deterministic, AST-derived): cyclomatic_complexity (int), cognitive_complexity (int), method_loc (int). These do NOT go in llm-annotations.json.

FieldNode.$comment: TODO (learner): fill in properties. Suggested: name, type (string), modifiers, annotations (string[]). Design question: does @Autowired appear here as an annotation string while the AUTOWIRES edge captures the semantic meaning? Or add is_autowired (bool) as a convenience? Decide and be consistent.

ContainsEdge.$comment: TODO (learner): does ContainsEdge need any properties beyond source/target/kind? e.g. ordinal position of a method within a class? Probably no for v1 — keep minimal.

source.description: ID of the containing node (module, class).

target.description: ID of the contained node (class, method, field).

CallsResolvedEdge.$comment: TODO (learner): add properties. Suggested: call_count (int — how many times this method calls the target within its body). Anything else the graph consumer needs?

target.description: Called method ID — resolved to a known node.

CallsUnresolvedEdge.$comment: TODO (learner): add properties. Suggested: raw_call_expr (string — the literal call expression from source, for debuggability). Consider: call_count here too, or one edge per callee name is enough?

target.description: Best-effort callee name. May not match any node ID in the graph.

DependsOnEdge.$comment: TODO (learner): does DependsOnEdge need properties beyond source/target/kind? Note: CBO is calculated by counting distinct depends_on targets per class — this edge as-is is sufficient for that. Keep minimal unless there is a clear need.

AutowiresEdge.$comment: TODO (learner): add properties. Suggested: injection_type (enum: field|constructor|setter) — the renderer needs this to generate correct DI wiring in the target language (constructor injection in Go/TS vs field injection).

source.description: Field ID carrying the injection annotation.
